sqlMap.select: select CATALOG_NAME from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.SCHEMATA where CATALOG_NAME = #dbname#
select * from sys.databases where name = #dbname#
select * from sys.databases where name = #dbname#
select name from sysobjects where xtype = 'F' and parent_obj=#id#
select name from sysobjects where xtype = 'TR' and parent_obj=#id#
select column_name from information_schema.key_column_usage where constraint_name like #keyType# + '%' and table_name=#name#
select name, id from sysobjects where xtype = 'U'
select name, id from sysobjects where xtype = 'U' and name = #name#

select b.colid, b.name name, c.name type, b.length length, b.prec, b.scale, b.autoval, b.isnullable from sysobjects a, syscolumns b, systypes c where a.id = #id# and a.id = b.id and b.xtype = c.xtype order by b.colorder

select b.column_id as colid, b.name name, c.name type, b.max_length length, b.precision, b.scale, b.is_identity as autoval, b.is_nullable as isnullable from sys.objects a, sys.columns b, sys.types c where a.object_id = #id# and a.object_id = b.object_id and b.user_type_id = c.user_type_id order by b.column_id

select b.column_id as colid, b.name name, c.name type, b.max_length length, b.precision, b.scale, b.is_identity as autoval, b.is_nullable as isnullable from sys.objects a, sys.columns b, sys.types c where a.object_id = #id# and a.object_id = b.object_id and b.user_type_id = c.user_type_id order by b.column_id

select c.colid, c.keyno from sysobjects a, sysindexes b, sysindexkeys c where a.parent_obj = #id# and a.parent_obj = b.id and a.xtype = 'PK' and a.name = b.name and c.id = a.parent_obj and c.indid = b.indid and c.colid = #colid# order by c.keyno

select c.colid, c.keyno from sys.objects a, sysindexes b, sysindexkeys c where a.parent_object_id = #id# and a.parent_object_id = b.id and a.type = 'PK' and a.name = b.name and c.id = a.parent_object_id and c.indid = b.indid and c.colid = #colid# order by c.keyno

select c.colid, c.keyno from sys.objects a, sysindexes b, sysindexkeys c where a.parent_object_id = #id# and a.parent_object_id = b.id and a.type = 'PK' and a.name = b.name and c.id = a.parent_object_id and c.indid = b.indid and c.colid = #colid# order by c.keyno
select distinct b.name, b.id from sysforeignkeys a, sysobjects b where a.rkeyid = #id# and b.id = a.fkeyid
select fkey from sysforeignkeys a, sysobjects b where a.rkeyid = #id# and b.id = a.fkeyid and b.id = #dpnd_id#
select distinct b.name, b.object_id as id from sysforeignkeys a, sys.objects b where a.rkeyid = #id# and b.object_id = a.fkeyid
select distinct b.name, b.object_id as id from sysforeignkeys a, sys.objects b where a.rkeyid = #id# and b.object_id = a.fkeyid
select fkey from sysforeignkeys a, sys.objects b where a.rkeyid = #id# and b.object_id = a.fkeyid and b.object_id = #dpnd_id#
select fkey from sysforeignkeys a, sys.objects b where a.rkeyid = #id# and b.object_id = a.fkeyid and b.object_id = #dpnd_id#
select a.[name], a.cdefault, b.[text] from syscolumns as a left join syscomments as b on b.[id] = a.[cdefault] where a.[name] = #name#

SELECT KCU.COLUMN_NAME FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLE_CONSTRAINTS AS TC INNER JOIN INFORMATION_SCHEMA.KEY_COLUMN_USAGE AS KCU ON TC.TABLE_SCHEMA = KCU.TABLE_SCHEMA AND TC.TABLE_NAME = KCU.TABLE_NAME AND TC.CONSTRAINT_NAME = KCU.CONSTRAINT_NAME WHERE TC.CONSTRAINT_TYPE = 'PRIMARY KEY' AND TC.TABLE_SCHEMA = 'dbo' AND TC.TABLE_NAME = #table_name# ORDER BY KCU.ORDINAL_POSITION
